summary: Scope `batch-g-data-ingestion-admin` is not implementation-ready yet. The planning bundle is close, but it still has blocking gaps in PRD formalization, legacy endpoint compatibility, and persistence-rule consistency. Readiness report: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/batch-g-data-ingestion-admin-implementation-readiness.md`. Findings: 3 critical, 2 major, 2 minor.

decisions.decision: Treat the operator-selected Batch G architecture and epics/stories artifacts as the authoritative planning bundle for this readiness review.
decisions.rationale: The operator explicitly scoped validation to the batch-g artifacts, and adjacent non-Batch-G planning files were treated as neighboring iterations rather than active duplicates.

decisions.decision: Use docs/discovery/data-ingestion-admin-brief.md as the de facto PRD input for traceability validation.
decisions.rationale: No formal PRD artifact exists for this scope under _bmad-output/planning-artifacts.

decisions.decision: Mark implementation readiness as needs-work rather than ready.
decisions.rationale: Critical documentation and contract gaps remain unresolved, and they are likely to cause implementation divergence if sprint execution starts now.

risks.title: No formal PRD artifact
risks.details: The planning bundle relies on the discovery brief plus later architecture decisions instead of a single frozen PRD source of truth.

risks.title: Legacy endpoint compatibility is underspecified
risks.details: The frozen v1 path /admin/alunos/{student_id}/indicadores/carga-inicial must remain compatible, but request mapping, response shape, error behavior, and migration window are not defined.

risks.title: Persistence semantics conflict across planning artifacts
risks.details: Batch G architecture preserves replace_enrollment semantics, while docs/admin-crud-spec.md says indicator ingestion should not delete historical measurements in the MVP.

risks.title: Rollback outcome recording is not operationally specified
risks.details: The bundle requires rollback status recording after operator-assisted restore, but it does not define how that status is persisted.

risks.title: Execution-detail frontend scope is inconsistent
risks.details: Architecture treats execution-detail UI as future enhancement, while Epic 3 Story 3.1 still implies current frontend rendering scope.

risks.title: Admin entry path can still be misinterpreted
risks.details: The feature is described as a new admin operation while also depending on selected-student context, which could lead to a second selection flow.

risks.title: Concurrency expectation is undocumented in acceptance criteria
risks.details: Architecture mentions a storage IO lock for apply, but the stories do not state the expected single-writer behavior.
